howItWorks.intro: This solar battery sizing calculator uses established formulas to provide accurate results.

rules.name: Battery Capacity
rules.formula: Total kWh = Daily Usage x Backup Days / Depth of Discharge
rules.explanation: The total battery capacity accounts for the fact that you should not fully discharge batteries.

howItWorks.outro: Energy costs and incentives change by region and season. Check with your local utility for current rates.

faq.q: How many batteries do I need for off-grid solar?
faq.a: It depends on your daily usage and backup days. A typical home using 30 kWh/day needs 60-75 kWh of battery capacity for 2 days of autonomy.

faq.q: Lithium vs lead-acid for solar storage?
faq.a: Lithium (LFP) costs more upfront but lasts 10+ years with deeper discharge. Lead-acid is cheaper initially but needs replacement every 3-5 years.
